CheckTitle: Ensure self-service password reset is enabled for all users
Description: Checks whether self-service password reset (SSPR) is enabled for all users or only a subset. When SSPR is not enabled, users must contact the helpdesk, which delays recovery and increases social engineering risk on helpdesk staff.
Risk: Without SSPR, helpdesk staff become a target for social engineering to reset passwords on behalf of attackers. SSPR with proper MFA reduces this attack vector and improves user productivity.

Remediation.Recommendation: Enable self-service password reset for all users in the Entra ID authentication methods policy. Require at least two authentication methods for SSPR verification.

SourceNotes: SSPR is a well-known identity hygiene best practice. CIS control ID is AI-generated.
